channel.title: The Sentinel Review — National Security Feed
channel.description: Automated national security news aggregation. Updated every 8 hours.

item.title: 🏛️ A message from Normandy, 82 years later | Breaking Defense
item.description: In remembrance of eight U.S. service members who received the Congressional Medal of Honor for their actions during the D-Day assault and the Normandy campaign that followed.

item.title: 🏛️ Navy lifts price ceiling for new trainer jet | Breaking Defense
item.description: The Navy updated the price ceiling to $2.7 billion in May, up from roughly $1.8 billion.

item.title: 🌍 India’s energy security at a crossroads: The Hormuz crisis and an opportunity for US-India cooperation | Atlantic Council
item.description: The Iran crisis has exposed India's energy vulnerabilities but opens an opportunity for a stronger trade partnership between the US and India. The post India’s energy security at a crossroads: The Hormuz crisis and an opportunity for US-India cooperation appeared first on…

item.title: 🛡️ Countering terrorist propaganda in the age of AI | Atlantic Council
item.description: More people are being exposed to violent propaganda online, and the time between seeing such content, recruitment by a terrorist organization, and action is getting shorter. Counter-terrorism today thus must mean limiting the spread of terrorist content. The post Countering…

item.title: 🌏 What in the World? | Foreign Policy
item.description: Test yourself on the week of May 30: Malta votes, Japan responds to militarism accusations, and Iran pauses talks with the U.S.

item.title: 🏛️ Under ‘Drone Dominance’ push, Pentagon begins receiving small drones | Breaking Defense
item.description: So far, the department has ordered 20,000 small, FPV-style drones from 10 vendors.

item.title: 📍 Myanmar’s Military Isn’t Conceding Much | Foreign Policy
item.description: Prisoner releases mostly reflect the regime’s growing confidence five years after seizing power in a coup.

item.title: 🏛️ Huge fascist-era underground cisterns draw Western special forces to Italy for tunnel training | Defense News
item.description: After resisting 150 bombing runs by the Allies in the Second World War, the cisterns continued to be used by the Italian Air Force to hold aviation fuel.

item.title: 🏴 Lockheed Martin UK-led consortium unveils GBAD concept for NATO | Breaking Defense
item.description: While company representatives were tight-lipped about the exact technical details of their offering, they explained that a flexible, software-based system would allow individual member-nations to connect their sensors to another nation’s command nodes.

item.title: 🌍 Sleeping in Mexico, Playing in America | Foreign Policy
item.description: The journey of Iran’s World Cup team sets a dangerous precedent for international soccer.

item.title: 📍 Innovation as resilience: Demand-side strategies for critical mineral supply chain security | Atlantic Council
item.description: Policies designed to secure critical mineral supplies have a blind spot: they focus primarily on sourcing but underuse demand-side strategies. The post Innovation as resilience: Demand-side strategies for critical mineral supply chain security appeared first on Atlantic Council.

item.title: 🏛️ DoD cyber strategy will set a ‘clear and specific vision’ for AI to enable the force: Official | Breaking Defense
item.description: Katie Sutton, Assistant Secretary of Defense for Cyber Policy and Principal Cyber Advisor, is looking to coordinate AI adoption across several entities within the Pentagon.

item.title: 🏛️ Confessions Of A Navy MH-53E Sea Dragon Minehunter Pilot | The War Zone
item.description: A former pilot gives an in-depth look at what it takes to fly and sweep for mines in the giant Sea Dragon, and shares some great stories along the way. The post Confessions Of A Navy MH-53E Sea Dragon Minehunter Pilot appeared first on The War Zone.

item.title: 🕵️ Agentic AI opens the door to weaponizing financial systems | Atlantic Council
item.description: AI-enabled financial operations are extending the bounds of modern irregular warfare, targeting systemic vulnerabilities rather than battlefield positions. The post Agentic AI opens the door to weaponizing financial systems appeared first on Atlantic Council.

item.title: 📍 HASC adopts FY27 defense policy bill, adds right to repair language | Breaking Defense
item.description: Rep. Maggie Goodlander, D-N.H., said the provisions would “streamline … processes, cut red tape, and close loopholes” that make it impossible for troops to repair equiprment.

item.title: 🏛️ A deeper look at Trump’s new policy toward the Western Balkans | Atlantic Council
item.description: Atlantic Council experts examine the recently released, congressionally mandated report on US policy toward the Western Balkans. The post A deeper look at Trump’s new policy toward the Western Balkans appeared first on Atlantic Council.

item.title: 📍 Does the United States Have the Weapons for Trade War? | Foreign Policy
item.description: Economic strategists are pitching tougher policies for an anxious world.

item.title: 📍 The Lessons of Zambia’s RightsCon Cancellation for International Democracy Promotion | Just Security
item.description: The once-lauded Zambian president's nixing of a major digital rights conference shows the risks of lionizing individual leaders without a backup plan. The post The Lessons of Zambia’s RightsCon Cancellation for International Democracy Promotion appeared first on Just Security.

item.title: 🔐 Trump Drew the Right Map for AI Oversight, but Stopped at the Trailhead | Just Security
item.description: Trump's new AI executive order creates the scaffolding for a workable regulatory regime, especially for cybersecurity risks, but failed to make it mandatory. The post Trump Drew the Right Map for AI Oversight, but Stopped at the Trailhead appeared first on Just Security.

item.title: 📍 Can the Secretary of Defense Remove Admirals from a Promotion List? | Just Security
item.description: The legal questions raised by these removals ultimately extend far beyond the careers of the officers involved. The post Can the Secretary of Defense Remove Admirals from a Promotion List? appeared first on Just Security.

item.title: 🏛️ US forces board sanctioned tanker in Indian Ocean, Pentagon says | Defense News
item.description: U.S. forces have intercepted multiple commercial and oil tankers in the Indian Ocean in recent months.

item.title: 🏛️ Legislators Demand Navy Prove Trump Class Battleships Won’t Sink U.S. Nuclear Shipbuilding | The War Zone
item.description: Concerns are building the Trump class battleship will exacerbate delays in critical aircraft carrier and submarine construction. The post Legislators Demand Navy Prove Trump Class Battleships Won’t Sink U.S. Nuclear Shipbuilding appeared first on The War Zone.

item.title: 📍 3 things to know about delayed US arms sales to allies | Breaking Defense
item.description: “The bottom line is the US is becoming less dependable as a supplier,” one analyst said.

item.title: ⚛️ The US Army wants thousands of air defense missiles to replace the Stinger | Defense News
item.description: A new Army Request for Information seeks to identify companies that can deliver 11,000 next-gen, short range interceptors beginning in Fiscal Year 2028.

item.title: 🌍 Early Edition: June 5, 2026 | Just Security
item.description: Signup to receive the Early Edition in your inbox here. A curated weekday guide to major news and developments over the last 24 hours. Here’s today’s news: IRAN WAR – LEBANON OPERATIONS Israeli airstrikes and Hezbollah rocket and drone attacks in southern Lebanon continued…

item.title: 📍 How Brazilian Soccer Shed Protectionism | Foreign Policy
item.description: Will openness to foreign players and coaches make a difference at the World Cup?

item.title: 🏴 UK running out of time to boost defense as investment plan stalls, military chief warns | Defense News
item.description: Defence Minister John Healey said on Monday that Prime Minister Keir Starmer was determined to publish it before a July 7 NATO summit.

item.title: 🏴 Chhangani’s piece on digital yuan cited in European Central Bank’s report on the international role of the euro | Atlantic Council
item.description: Read full report here The post Chhangani’s piece on digital yuan cited in European Central Bank’s report on the international role of the euro appeared first on Atlantic Council.

item.title: 📍 Lipsky joined the IMF’s podcast on the Geoeconomics | Atlantic Council
item.description: Listen to full podcast here The post Lipsky joined the IMF’s podcast on the Geoeconomics appeared first on Atlantic Council.

item.title: 🌏 Chhangani’s CIPS data cited in South China Morning Post | Atlantic Council
item.description: Read full article here The post Chhangani’s CIPS data cited in South China Morning Post appeared first on Atlantic Council.
